REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS DEAN CLEMENTS, STACIE CLEMENTS, PAUL MCKENNA, AND JAMES GALLAGHER'S MOTION TO STRIKE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS SUBJECT TO THIS MOTION ARE BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS AND PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO ALLEGE FACTS SHOWING EQUITABLE TOLLING. PLAINTIFFS' OWN RENT BOARD PETITION WAS NOT FILED UNTIL SEPTEMBER 19, 2016. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT PARTICIPATION IN THE RENT BOARD PROCEEDINGS REGARDING DEFENDANT'S PETITION FOR RENT INCREASE WAS ONE OF MULTIPLE LEGAL REMEDIES AVAILABLE TO PLAINTIFFS AND PURSUED IN GOOD FAITH IN ORDER TO OBTAIN RELIEF TO THE SAME CLAIMS THAT ARE ALLEGED IN THIS ACTION. PLAINTIFFS DID NOT OPPOSE DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STRIKE "THE 'PROCEDURAL HISTORY' SECTION" NOTICE OF MOTION 1:28 AND "PARAGRAPH 9 OF THE COMMON ALLEGATIONS." ID. AT 2:1. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: BARRIE HART, CSR 6954 (BAY AREA TRIAL REPORTERS, TEL: 415.328.6644)

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Oct-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS DEAN CLEMENTS, STACIE CLEMENTS, PAUL MCKENNA, AND JAMES GALLAGHER'S MOTION TO STRIKE

Appearances: Michele Scott of the Law Offices of Ed Singer (650.814.8479) for defendants and moving parties; Jesse C. Ralph (415.776.1826) for plaintiff and responding party.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: GRANTED. Plaintiffs' claims subject to this motion are barred by the statute of limitations and Plaintiffs failed to allege facts showing equitable tolling. Plaintiffs' own Rent Board Petition was not filed until September 19, 2016. Plaintiffs fail to cite any authority for the proposition that participation in the Rent Board proceedings regarding Defendant's Petition for rent increase was one of multiple legal remedies available to Plaintiffs and pursued in good faith in order to obtain relief to the same claims that are alleged in this action. Plaintiffs did not oppose Defendants' motion to strike "The 'Procedural History' section" Notice of Motion 1:28 and "Paragraph 9 of the Common Allegations." Id. at 2:1. Order signed. Judge: A. James Robertson, II, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Barrie Hart, CSR 6954 (Bay Area Trial Reporters, Tel: 415.328.6644) (501/AJR)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-04-2017 Continued To Nov-01-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-06-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jesse C. Ralph (415-776-1826) for Plaintiff/ opposing party Amit Dow.
Michele Scott (650-814-8479) for Defendants/Moving parties Dean Clements, Stacie Clements, Paul McKenna, and James Gallagher.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: Statute of limitations, damages, equitable tolling, rent board decision, Addison, intentional infliction of emotional distress, typographical error in year (March 22, 2015). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS DEAN CLEMENTS, STACIE CLEMENTS, PAUL MCKENNA, JAMES GALLAGHER'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is taken under submission. Defense to submit order to include language that the statute of limitations has run as indicated re: particular causes of action. Defense submits proposed order. Plaintiff to submit order to include language re: equitable tolling. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)
